title: Israeli Strikes Kill 7 Palestinians; Naval Fire Targets IDP Tents in Beit Lahia
detail: Israeli forces killed at least 7 Palestinians and wounded 22 others across the Gaza Strip on April 22, 2026, according to the Palestinian news agency WAFA and IMEMC. Among the most notable incidents, Israeli naval forces opened fire on displaced persons' tents in the Al-Salatin area, west of Beit Lahia in northern Gaza, killing civilian Hiba Jamal Abu Shuqfa. Rescue operations were hampered by ongoing fire, destroyed roads, and lack of heavy machinery. The cumulative Palestinian death toll since the October 10, 2025 ceasefire framework took effect has now risen to at least 784 killed and 2,214 wounded, with over 2,400 Israeli ceasefire violations documented by Gaza's Government Media Office. The total Palestinian death toll since October 7, 2023 has surpassed 72,560 confirmed killed (Gaza Ministry of Health / WAFA, April 22, 2026).

media.caption: Gaza sees new casualties; overall toll since October 2025 ceasefire reaches 784 killed

title: Six Months After Ceasefire Announcement: OHCHR Says 784+ Palestinians Killed, Gaza Still Unsafe
detail: UN High Commissioner for Human Rights Volker Türk issued a statement marking approximately six months since the October 10, 2025 ceasefire announcement, declaring that 'Palestinians across Gaza remain unsafe.' At least 784 Palestinians have been killed and 2,214 wounded since the ceasefire took effect — an average of more than four people killed per day. Türk noted that 'incidents of Palestinians killed by Israeli forces while walking, driving, or standing outside are recorded nearly every day,' and that movement itself has become 'a life-threatening activity.' Separately, a UN Women April 2026 report documented that more than 38,000 women and girls were killed in Gaza between October 2023 and December 2025 — an average of 47 per day. The 2026 Flash Appeal for Gaza remains only 9.7% funded at $4.1 billion requested, and aid is reaching only 37% of required levels. The EU-UN joint reconstruction assessment released April 21 found that Gaza needs $71.4 billion over the next decade, with human development set back 77 years.

media.caption: OHCHR: Palestinians across Gaza unsafe six months after ceasefire announcement — 784 killed
